subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze Janu’s internal conflict between tradition and her desire for education
Explore the symbolism of the river as a source of wisdom and courage
Examine gender roles and social barriers in rural settings
Identify and use figurative language (personification and metaphors)
Practice inferential reading and character mapping
Construct arguments for the importance of girl-child education

lesson_plan.title: Context and Symbolism
topics_covered: Geographic and social setting of the story
Introduction to Janu’s character profile
The concept of personification in nature
teaching_strategy: Visual presentation of riverside village life
Deep-dive discussion on the title's metaphorical meaning
Predictive mapping: What could the river represent to Janu?
student_activities: Create a 'Character Web' for Janu including her dreams and fears
Identify personification examples in the opening paragraphs

lesson_plan.title: Social Barriers and Dreams
topics_covered: Janu’s longing for school vs. her household duties
The disparity between male and female opportunities
Key vocabulary: 'monotony', 'yearning', 'defiance'
teaching_strategy: Comparative reading of Janu’s day vs. her brothers' day
Socratic questioning about fairness and tradition
Textual evidence finding (finding quotes that show Janu's sadness)
student_activities: T-Chart: Chores vs. Education opportunities
Write a diary entry from Janu’s perspective expressing her frustration

lesson_plan.title: The River’s Philosophy
topics_covered: The river as a silent teacher
Interpreting the 'speech' of the river
Imagery and sensory details in the narrative
teaching_strategy: Guided analysis of the river’s movement as a metaphor for progress
Teacher-led exploration of the 'voices' Janu hears
Discussion on resilience (how water overcomes obstacles)
student_activities: Identify sensory words (sight, sound, touch) in the river descriptions
Pair-share: What advice is the river giving Janu?

lesson_plan.title: Internal Conflict and Climax
topics_covered: Janu’s decision to act
The influence of the Arabic teacher or community elders
Themes of self-reliance and grit
teaching_strategy: Debate: Should Janu follow tradition or her heart?
Analysis of the turning point in the story
Discussion on the importance of mentors
student_activities: Role-play: Janu presenting her case to her parents
Flowchart showing Janu’s emotional journey from sadness to hope

lesson_plan.title: Resolution and Empowerment
topics_covered: The ending: A new beginning for Janu
Connecting the story to real-world issues (Beti Bachao Beti Padhao)
Moral and social implications of the story
teaching_strategy: Final thematic wrap-up
Connection to modern-day examples of girls overcoming barriers
Reflective questioning on the value of literacy
student_activities: Write a letter to Janu 10 years in the future
Create a poster advocating for equal education

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Language Workshop
topics_covered: Phrasal verbs in the context of the story
Complex sentence structures
Vocabulary revision through context clues
teaching_strategy: Grammar workshop focusing on descriptive adverbs
Interactive quiz on story details and literary devices
Correction of common errors in narrative writing
student_activities: Sentence transformation exercises
Vocabulary crossword based on 'The Day the River Spoke'

teaching_aids: Excerpt from 'I Am Malala' (for thematic comparison)
teacher_tip: Push students to look beyond the literal text. Ask them 'Why' Janu feels the river speaks, moving them toward understanding the river as a projection of her own inner strength and wisdom.
